title: rosa canina subsp. spuria
intro: Rosa canina subsp. spuria, also known as the wild rose of the Alps or the Pyrenees rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa canina plant, which is a type of wild rose native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Rosa canina subsp. spuria include soothing digestive problems, reducing inflammation and pain, and supporting immune function, among others. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
